compositional_truth_check.p1_yellow_pot: Systemic Loss: No visible plant matter; soil appears dormant/barren.
compositional_truth_check.p2_black_pot: Systemic Loss: No visible plant matter; soil appears dormant/barren.
compositional_truth_check.p3_black_pot: Present: Pothos specimen with single leaf and white rabbit anchor.
compositional_truth_check.p4_black_pot: Systemic Loss: No visible plant matter; soil appears dormant/barren.

inventory_reconciliation.notes: Registry indicates four distinct specimens, but visual evidence confirms only one surviving Pothos (P3). P1, P2, and P4 show no signs of life, suggesting either total senescence or complete loss of biomass.

plant_audit.p3: Single leaf Pothos, stable posture, healthy turgor.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Dark, moist, consistent across all pots.
biome_observations.anomalies: Presence of crushed eggshells in P3/P4 area; electronic component (IC chip) introduced on desk surface in later frames.
biome_observations.debris: White paper/receipt fragment introduced in T-4.

temporal_deltas.t4_to_t3: Introduction of electronic component (IC chip) on the desk.
temporal_deltas.t3_to_current: Static state; no further biological growth or decline observed.

visual_health_inference.p3_pothos: Stable. The single leaf maintains its structural integrity and color, indicating adequate hydration and light levels.
visual_health_inference.others: Deceased/Lost. The absence of stems or foliage in P1, P2, and P4 indicates these specimens are no longer viable.

vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is currently dominated by a single Pothos specimen (P3). The other registered plants (P1, P2, P4) have suffered systemic loss, leaving the pots barren. The environment has been modified by the introduction of non-biological items, specifically an electronic component and paper debris, which do not impact the Pothos but indicate human activity in the immediate vicinity.
